originalTitle: 'I like their money': Trump threatens lawsuits against ABC for reporting on Reflecting Pool
summary: The latest action against ABC comes as the broadcaster faces two investigations from the Federal Communications Commission.
content:
U.S. President Donald Trump has threatened to sue broadcaster ABC for its reporting on the cost of repairing damage sustained by the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington D.C. Trump said the network had not reported costs incurred by previous administrations in maintaining the landmark.

In a post on Truth Social, Trump claimed that ABC "failed to report that their close 'friends,' Dumocrats Obama and Biden, spent over 100 Million Dollars on the Reflecting Pool, and it never worked."

Trump did not give any evidence for the $100 million claim, but prior reporting by PBS indicated that former President Barack Obama's administration spent around $35 million on repairs for the monument. No major repairs to the pool are known to have been carried out during the Biden administration.

He also claimed that the damage to the monument has been caused by vandalism, but no evidence that the Reflecting Pool was intentionally damaged has been provided by the White House. Trump claimed to reporters on Monday that a "350-foot slit" was cut with a "box-cutter or a knife of some kind." NBC News reported that at least five people have been arrested in connection with the alleged vandalism.

"We are preparing lawsuits against ABC for false reporting. I like their money, which will be given to the U.S. Treasury," Trump added, also referencing $16 million that ABC paid to settle a defamation case he filed in 2024.

The network paid $15 million, which was put toward Trump's presidential library, and an additional $1 million in legal fees.

Last week, ABC reported that the cost to repaint the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool had ballooned to more than $14.65 million. This exceeded the original estimated cost of the no-bid contract — that was not awarded by competitive tender — by more than $4 million, according to federal contract data.

The broadcaster also highlighted a $1.74 million contract awarded to Green Water Solutions, an Ohio-based company, to install a "nano bubble" system to kill algae, using a similar no-bid process. This would have brought the cost of the entire project to more than $16 million.

The latest action against ABC comes as the company faces two investigations from the Federal Communications Commission, led by Trump appointee Brendan Carr. The FCC also demanded that ABC submit early renewal applications for eight of its local broadcasting licenses. That move came soon after comedian Jimmy Kimmel made a joke about first lady Melania Trump and the U.S. president called for Kimmel's firing.

The stations were reportedly not scheduled to apply for renewal until 2028 at the earliest.

On Monday, ABC said it was launching an on-air campaign encouraging viewers to support the network after its daytime talk show "The View" was investigated under federal "equal time" rules for ​political candidates, Reuters reported.
chineseTitle: “我喜欢他们的钱”：特朗普威胁对美国广播公司（ABC）提起诉讼，因为它报道了《倒影池》
chineseSummary: 针对美国广播公司的最新行动正值该广播公司面临联邦通信委员会的两项调查之际。
chineseContent:
美国总统唐纳德·特朗普威胁要起诉美国广播公司（ABC），因为该公司报道了修复华盛顿特区林肯纪念堂倒影池受损的费用。特朗普表示，该电视台没有报告前几届政府维护这一地标所产生的费用。

特朗普在《Truth Social》上发表的一篇文章中声称，ABC“未能报道他们的亲密‘朋友’杜民主党人奥巴马和拜登在倒影池上花费了超过 1 亿美元，而且从未奏效。”

特朗普没有就这一 1 亿美元的索赔提供任何证据，但 PBS 之前的报道显示，前总统巴拉克·奥巴马政府花费了约 3500 万美元用于修复这座纪念碑。据了解，拜登执政期间没有对泳池进行过重大维修。

他还声称纪念碑的损坏是人为破坏造成的，但白宫没有提供任何证据表明倒影池是被故意损坏的。特朗普周一向记者声称，用“美工刀或某种刀”切出了一条“350英尺长的裂缝”。美国全国广播公司新闻报道称，至少有五人因涉嫌破坏公物而被捕。

特朗普补充道：“我们正在准备对 ABC 提起虚假报道诉讼。我喜欢他们的钱，这些钱将交给美国财政部。”他还提到 ABC 为和解他在 2024 年提起的诽谤案支付了 1600 万美元。

该网络支付了 1500 万美元，用于捐赠特朗普的总统图书馆，另外还支付了 100 万美元的法律费用。

上周，美国广播公司 (ABC) 报道称，重新粉刷林肯纪念堂倒影池的成本已飙升至超过 1,465 万美元。根据联邦合同数据，这超出了无投标合同（并非通过竞争性招标授予）的最初估计成本超过 400 万美元。

该广播公司还强调了与俄亥俄州 Green Water Solutions 公司签订的价值 174 万美元的合同，该合同使用类似的无投标流程安装“纳米气泡”系统来杀死藻类。这将使整个项目的成本超过 1600 万美元。

针对美国广播公司采取的最新行动正值该公司面临联邦通信委员会的两项调查，调查由特朗普任命的布伦丹·卡尔领导。 FCC 还要求 ABC 提前提交八个当地广播许可证的续签申请。喜剧演员吉米·金梅尔(Jimmy Kimmel)拿第一夫人梅拉尼娅·特朗普(Melania Trump)开玩笑，美国总统呼吁解雇金梅尔后不久，就采取了这一举措。

据报道，这些车站最早要到 2028 年才会申请续约。

据路透社报道，美国广播公司 (ABC) 周一表示，在其日间脱口秀节目“The View”根据联邦政治候选人“平等时间”规则进行调查后，该公司正在发起一场直播活动，鼓励观众支持该网络。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
